title: Nature of Cumulative Data
text: Cumulative data, by definition, adds up over time. Think about whether a sinusoidal function, which oscillates, can accurately represent a quantity that only increases (or decreases monotonically).

hintAnswer: The total amount of rainfall collected up to a certain point in time
title: Define Cumulative
text: What does 'cumulative' mean in the context of $$rainfall$$?
choices: The total amount of rainfall collected up to a certain point in time
The rate at which rain falls

title: Sinusoidal Function Behavior
text: Recall that sinusoidal functions go up and down, repeating their values. Does cumulative rainfall ever decrease over the course of a year?

title: Analyze Sinusoidal Pattern
text: If a function models cumulative $$rainfall$$, should its graph ever go downwards?
choices: Only if there is a drought
